# Mohamed Alhaskir

> Mohamed Alhaskir is a Syrian-German machine learning researcher and engineer, and a PhD candidate at the Institute of Medical Informatics, RWTH Aachen University (Aachen, Germany). His research focuses on multimodal, LLM-powered AI for medicine — building trustworthy, privacy-preserving, and clinically deployable systems.

## About

- **Name:** Mohamed Alhaskir
- **Role:** PhD Candidate · Multimodal AI for Medicine · Machine Learning Researcher
- **Affiliation:** Institute of Medical Informatics, RWTH Aachen University, Aachen, Germany
- **Nationality:** Syrian-German
- **Website:** https://mohamed-alhaskir.github.io/
- **Email:** malhaskir@ukaachen.de

## Research focus

- Multimodal large language models and generative AI for medicine
- Trustworthy AI in clinical settings (robustness, reproducibility, auditability)
- Privacy-preserving machine learning for sensitive medical data
- Dependable, deployable clinical AI systems and reproducible workflows
- Clinical AI evaluation and biosignal processing

## Selected publications

- Reliable Detection of Focal Onset Impaired Awareness Seizures Using Wearable ECG (Alhaskir, M., et al., Computer Methods and Programs in Biomedicine, 2026)
- ECG Matching: Synchronizing ECG Datasets for Data Quality Comparisons (Alhaskir, M., et al., Studies in Health Technology and Informatics, 2023)
- The Role of Self-Supervised Pretraining in Differentially Private Medical Image Analysis (co-author, arXiv preprint, 2026)

## Open-source projects

- HaMoJo — multimodal LLM-powered system for clinical skills assessment in pediatric simulation training: https://github.com/Mohamed-Alhaskir/HaMoJo
- MoniPy — open-source ML pipeline used as a framework for trustworthy clinical-AI research on biosignal data: https://github.com/rwth-imi/monipy

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mohamed-alhaskir

## Awards

- DAAD Prize for Outstanding International Students, RWTH Aachen (2021)
